proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) have a lower objective_response rate than patients with lower ECOG performance status.
Older patients (age_years) have a different objective_response rate than younger patients.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different objective_response rate than male patients.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import pointbiserialr; pointbiserialr(df['ecog_ps'], df['objective_response'])
analyses.result_summary: Higher ECOG_PS associated with lower ORR (point-biserial r=-0.0906, p=1.0e-91). ORR drops from ~22% at ECOG 0 to ~14% at ECOG 2.
No detectable association between age and objective response (r=-0.0007, p=0.88).
ORR 18.91% in females vs 18.62% in males (chi-square p=0.42).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with NPM1 mutation (npm1_mutation=1) have a higher objective_response rate than patients without NPM1 mutation.
Patients with TP53 mutation (tp53_mutation=1) have a lower objective_response rate than patients without TP53 mutation.
Patients with complex karyotype (complex_karyotype=1) have a lower objective_response rate than patients without complex karyotype.
Patients with secondary AML (secondary_aml=1) have a lower objective_response rate than patients with de novo AML.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ ORR 23.46% vs NPM1- ORR 16.73%; difference +0.067, chi-square p=5.8e-70.
TP53+ ORR 16.57% vs TP53- ORR 18.99%; difference -0.024, chi-square p=3.5e-5.
Complex karyotype ORR 16.81% vs non-complex 19.09%; difference -0.023, chi-square p=3.3e-6.
Secondary AML ORR 18.81% vs de novo 18.73%; no difference detected (p=0.85).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with FLT3-ITD mutation (flt3_itd=1) have a different objective_response rate than patients without FLT3-ITD.
Patients with FLT3-TKD mutation (flt3_tkd=1) have a different objective_response rate than patients without FLT3-TKD.
Patients with IDH1 mutation (idh1_mutation=1) have a different objective_response rate than patients without IDH1 mutation.
Patients with IDH2 mutation (idh2_mutation=1) have a different objective_response rate than patients without IDH2 mutation.

analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD+ ORR 18.92% vs FLT3-ITD- 18.71%; no significant difference (p=0.64).
FLT3-TKD+ ORR 18.05% vs FLT3-TKD- 18.79%; no significant difference (p=0.34).
IDH1+ ORR 19.09% vs IDH1- 18.72%; no significant difference (p=0.60).
IDH2+ ORR 19.15% vs IDH2- 18.71%; no significant difference (p=0.47).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients labeled unfit_for_intensive (unfit_for_intensive=1) have a different objective_response rate than fit patients.
Higher marrow blast percentage (blast_pct_marrow) is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher peripheral white blood cell count (wbc_k_per_ul) is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with a higher objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: unfit_for_intensive=1 ORR 22.10% vs unfit_for_intensive=0 ORR 16.51%; difference +0.056, p=1.9e-55. Counterintuitive direction; later analyses (iterations 7-10) show this is driven entirely by venetoclax-azacitidine response in this subgroup.
Higher blast% → lower ORR (r=-0.0367, p=2.2e-16). Top quartile ORR 16.6% vs bottom quartile 20.7%.
Higher WBC → modestly lower ORR (r=-0.0167, p=1.8e-4).
Higher albumin → higher ORR (r=+0.0230, p=2.8e-7).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher serum CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with a different objective_response rate.
Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with a different objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Greater weight loss → lower ORR (r=-0.0516, p=8.0e-31). Top quartile ORR 15.7% vs bottom 20.5%.
Higher CRP → lower ORR (r=-0.0202, p=6.0e-6).
No detectable association (r=-0.0042, p=0.35).
No detectable association (r=-0.0005, p=0.92).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.
Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a different objective_response rate than those who do not.

analyses.result_summary: ven-aza ORR 21.96% vs no ven-aza 16.61%; difference +0.054, p=6.6e-51.
7+3 ORR 18.89% vs no 7+3 18.63%; no marginal benefit (p=0.46).
midostaurin ORR 18.57% vs none 18.78%; no marginal effect (p=0.67).
gilteritinib ORR 18.13% vs none 18.86%; no significant marginal effect (p=0.14).
ivosidenib ORR 18.82% vs none 18.74%; no marginal effect (p=0.93).
enasidenib ORR 18.58% vs none 18.76%; no marginal effect (p=0.79).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all available baseline features and treatments, the multivariable-adjusted association of NPM1 mutation with objective_response remains positive and significant.
After adjusting for all baseline features, the adjusted effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is positive.

After multivariable adjustment, the targeted small-molecule treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) and treatment_7plus3 each have an adjusted main effect on objective_response that is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1 multivariable adjusted log-OR = +0.4355 (SE 0.024, z=17.88, p<1e-70).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine adjusted log-OR = +0.3617 (SE 0.023, z=15.44, p<1e-50).

Adjusted log-ORs (p-values): midostaurin -0.009 (p=0.79), gilteritinib -0.050 (p=0.13), ivosidenib +0.019 (p=0.65), enasidenib -0.011 (p=0.80), 7+3 +0.014 (p=0.55). No targeted-therapy or 7+3 main effect after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with FLT3-ITD mutation, the FLT3 inhibitor treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response, whereas in FLT3-ITD-negative patients it has no effect (i.e., midostaurin × flt3_itd interaction).
Among patients with FLT3-ITD mutation, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response, whereas in FLT3-ITD-negative patients it has no effect (gilteritinib × flt3_itd interaction).
Among patients with IDH1 mutation, treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response (ivosidenib × idh1_mutation interaction).
Among patients with IDH2 mutation, treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response (enasidenib × idh2_mutation interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Within FLT3-ITD+: midostaurin ORR 17.65% vs no-midostaurin 19.15% (TE = -0.015, p=0.18). Within FLT3-ITD-: TE = +0.001 (p=0.86). No biomarker-matched benefit; if anything a non-significant negative trend in FLT3-ITD+.
Within FLT3-ITD+: gilteritinib TE = +0.002 (p=0.83). Within FLT3-ITD-: TE = -0.010 (p=0.08). No biomarker-matched benefit of gilteritinib.
Within IDH1+: ivosidenib TE = -0.040 (p=0.11). Within IDH1-: TE = +0.004 (p=0.55). Direction is opposite to expectation; no significant benefit.
Within IDH2+: enasidenib TE = +0.004 (p=0.89). Within IDH2-: TE = -0.002 (p=0.74). No biomarker-matched benefit of enasidenib.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is larger in NPM1-mutated than in NPM1-wild-type patients (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation interaction).

The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is larger in unfit_for_intensive=1 than in unfit_for_intensive=0 patients (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × unfit_for_intensive interaction).
The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is abolished or reduced in patients with TP53 mutation (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × tp53_mutation interaction).
The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is abolished or reduced in patients with complex karyotype (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × complex_karyotype interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+: ven-aza TE = +0.187 (p=3.7e-152, n=15014). Within NPM1-: TE = -0.004 (p=0.34, n=34986). Massive interaction: ven-aza benefit is essentially confined to NPM1-mutated patients.
Within unfit+: ven-aza TE = +0.145 (p=8.9e-128, n=20056). Within unfit-: TE = -0.008 (p=0.08). Strong interaction: ven-aza benefit is concentrated in unfit_for_intensive patients.
Within TP53+: ven-aza TE = -0.004 (p=0.76, n=4985). Within TP53-: TE = +0.060 (p=3.6e-56). TP53 abrogates the benefit.
Within complex karyotype: ven-aza TE = +0.012 (p=0.20, n=7462). Within non-complex: TE = +0.061 (p=1.3e-54). Complex karyotype substantially attenuates the ven-aza benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint subgroup hypothesis: among patients who are NPM1-mutated AND unfit_for_intensive AND TP53-wild-type, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a much larger increase in objective_response than in any other subgroup.

Among NPM1-mutated AND unfit_for_intensive patients, the benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is suppressed both by TP53 mutation and by complex karyotype (each makes the treatment effect ≈ 0).

analyses.code: stratify ORR(venaza) by npm1 × unfit × tp53
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+ AND unfit+ AND TP53-: ven-aza TE = +0.534 (ORR 0.69 vs 0.16, n=5349, p≈0). Within NPM1+ AND unfit+ AND TP53+: TE = +0.006 (p=0.92, n=637). All other 6 cells of the 2×2×2 split show TE within ±0.013 of zero.

analyses.code: stratify ORR(venaza) within NPM1+ unfit+ by TP53 and CK
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+ unfit+ TP53- complex_karyotype-: TE = +0.628 (n=4531, p≈0). Within NPM1+ unfit+ TP53- complex_karyotype+: TE = +0.033 (n=818, p=0.21). Within NPM1+ unfit+ TP53+: TE = +0.006 (n=637). Both TP53 mutation and complex karyotype independently abolish the venetoclax-azacitidine effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint subgroup hypothesis (refined): among patients who are NPM1-mutated AND unfit_for_intensive AND TP53-wild-type AND lack a complex karyotype, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces an objective response in roughly 78% of patients vs ~16% off treatment (TE ≈ +0.63 absolute), and outside this composite subgroup the average treatment effect of ven-aza is ≈ 0.

analyses.result_summary: In responder subgroup (n=4531): ORR(ven-aza)=78.6% vs ORR(off)=15.8%, TE=+0.628, Fisher p≈0. Outside subgroup (n=45469): ORR(ven-aza)=16.6%, ORR(off)=16.7%, TE=-0.003 (p=0.42). The subgroup explains essentially all of the marginal ven-aza effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within NPM1+ AND TP53- AND complex_karyotype-, the unfit_for_intensive=1 predicate is necessary for the ven-aza benefit (i.e., in fit/eligible patients with the same molecular profile, ven-aza confers no benefit).

analyses.code: compare ORR(venaza) within NPM1+ TP53- CK- by unfit
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+ TP53- CK- unfit-: TE = -0.004 (n=6951). Within same molecular profile but unfit+: TE = +0.628 (n=4531). The unfit_for_intensive=1 predicate is a necessary component of the responder subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding treatment_7plus3 on top of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine within the responder subgroup does NOT further increase objective_response.
Within the responder subgroup, treatment_7plus3 alone (without ven-aza) does NOT increase objective_response.

analyses.code: within responder subgroup AND ven-aza=1, compare ORR by 7+3
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup AND ven-aza=1 (n=1790): ORR with 7+3 = 77.78% vs ORR without 7+3 = 79.31%; difference -0.015, n.s.

analyses.code: within responder subgroup, compare ORR(7+3) marginally
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup (n=4531): ORR(7+3=1) = 40.4% vs ORR(7+3=0) = 40.8%; TE = -0.004, n.s. The benefit there comes entirely from ven-aza, not 7+3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Formal logistic regression with full 4-way interaction among treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation × unfit_for_intensive × tp53_mutation will show a significant positive 3-way interaction (t × NPM1 × unfit) and a significant negative 4-way interaction (t × NPM1 × unfit × TP53), confirming TP53 cancels the joint NPM1+/unfit+ ven-aza response.

analyses.result_summary: 3-way ven-aza × NPM1 × unfit log-OR = +2.422 (SE 0.111, z=21.80, p<1e-100). 4-way ven-aza × NPM1 × unfit × TP53 log-OR = -2.640 (SE 0.356, z=-7.41, p=1.3e-13). Confirms NPM1+/unfit+ amplifies ven-aza response and TP53 cancels it.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment assignment is approximately random with respect to baseline biomarkers (i.e., the ven-aza × subgroup interactions reflect true effect modification rather than confounded treatment selection).

analyses.code: for each treatment, mean(biomarker) on vs off
analyses.result_summary: Across all six treatments and the key biomarkers (npm1, flt3_itd, idh1, idh2, tp53, unfit_for_intensive), mean differences between treated and untreated patients are |Δ| ≤ 0.016 in every cell, consistent with effectively random/unrelated treatment assignment. Thus the dramatic interactions are not due to confounded biomarker–treatment correlations.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Systematic 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_midostaurin will fail to identify a positive subgroup with absolute treatment effect ≥ 0.05 and p < 0.01.
Systematic 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_gilteritinib will fail to identify a positive subgroup with absolute treatment effect ≥ 0.05 and p < 0.01.
Systematic 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_ivosidenib will fail to identify a positive subgroup with absolute treatment effect ≥ 0.05 and p < 0.01.
Systematic 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_enasidenib will fail to identify a positive subgroup with absolute treatment effect ≥ 0.05 and p < 0.01.
Systematic 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_7plus3 will fail to identify a positive subgroup with absolute treatment effect ≥ 0.05 and p < 0.01.

analyses.result_summary: No subgroup achieved |TE|>0 with p<0.01. Best result for midostaurin had TE ≈ 0.0 (n.s.). No FLT3-conditional benefit detected.
Best subgroup TE = -0.018 (idh1=0 & unfit=0, n=27757, p=4.2e-3). All significant signals were modest negatives; no positive subgroup with |TE|≥0.05.
Best signal TE = -0.119 in idh2=1 & secondary_aml=1 (n=1141, p=8.4e-3). Direction is wrong (negative). No biomarker-matched positive subgroup detected.
Best (modest) positive: TE = +0.050 in npm1=1 & sex_female=1 (n=6689, ORR 27.9% vs 22.9%, p=9.4e-3). Marginal; not the IDH2-mutated subgroup that biology predicts.
Best signal TE = -0.043 in npm1=1 & complex_karyotype=1 (n=2273, p=5.7e-3). Direction is negative. No positive subgroup with |TE|≥0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within FLT3-ITD+ patients, neither treatment_midostaurin nor treatment_gilteritinib increases ORR beyond background — i.e., FLT3 inhibitors do not produce a measurable response benefit even in their canonical biomarker-matched subgroup.
Within IDH1+ patients, treatment_ivosidenib does not increase ORR; within IDH2+ patients, treatment_enasidenib does not increase ORR.

analyses.result_summary: Within FLT3-ITD+: midostaurin TE = -0.015 (p=0.18); gilteritinib TE = +0.002 (p=0.83). Neither FLT3 inhibitor improves ORR in FLT3-ITD+ patients.
Within IDH1+: ivosidenib TE = -0.040 (p=0.11). Within IDH2+: enasidenib TE = +0.004 (p=0.89). Neither IDH inhibitor improves ORR in its biomarker-matched subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the venetoclax-azacitidine responder subgroup (NPM1+ AND unfit+ AND TP53- AND CK-), the response benefit is preserved across ECOG performance status strata 0/1/2.

analyses.code: stratify TE(venaza) within responder subgroup by ECOG_PS
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, ven-aza TE by ECOG: ECOG 0 → +0.552, ECOG 1 → +0.529, ECOG 2 → +0.506. Effect is large and consistent across ECOG strata; ECOG modulates baseline response (lower at high ECOG) but not the treatment effect size.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for ECOG, blast %, weight loss, CRP, WBC, albumin, NPM1, TP53, complex karyotype, and other covariates, the four-way joint indicator I(ven-aza × NPM1 × unfit × ¬TP53 × ¬CK) has a large positive adjusted log-odds-ratio for objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ covariates + venaza + npm1 + unfit + I(venaza*npm1*unfit*(1-tp53)*(1-ck))', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR for the 4-way interaction term I(ven-aza × NPM1 × unfit × TP53-wt × non-CK) = +3.062 (SE 0.069, z=44.6, p<1e-300). Marginal ven-aza, NPM1, unfit terms collapse to ~0 once the joint indicator is in the model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sex (sex_female) does not modify the effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine within the NPM1+/unfit+/TP53-/CK- responder subgroup.
Age (age_years dichotomized at 70) does not modify the effect of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine within the NPM1+/unfit+/TP53-/CK- responder subgroup.

analyses.code: stratify TE(venaza) within responder subgroup by sex_female
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, ven-aza TE: sex_female=1 → +0.624; sex_female=0 → +0.631. Equal effect by sex.

analyses.code: stratify TE(venaza) within responder subgroup by age≥70
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, ven-aza TE is essentially identical across age halves (≈+0.63 in both <70 and ≥70 patients). Age does not modify the effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the responder subgroup, no other binary biomarker (FLT3-ITD, FLT3-TKD, IDH1, IDH2, secondary_aml) further amplifies the ven-aza effect to a clinically meaningful degree.

analyses.code: stratify ven-aza TE outside responder subgroup by each remaining binary feature
analyses.result_summary: Outside the NPM1+/unfit+/TP53-/CK- responder subgroup, ven-aza TE remains within ±0.02 of zero for every stratum of FLT3-ITD, FLT3-TKD, IDH1, IDH2, and secondary_aml. None of these markers identifies a second responder pocket.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher hemoglobin, alkaline phosphatase, AST, ALT, total bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, and calcium are NOT associated with objective_response after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted p-values: hemoglobin 0.19, alkaline phosphatase 0.023 (small +), AST 0.08, ALT 0.15, total bilirubin 0.22, creatinine 0.68, BUN 0.55, sodium 0.73, potassium 0.69, calcium 0.90. Apart from a marginal alkaline phosphatase signal (effect tiny), none of these chemistries shows a robust independent association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, the absolute response rate to treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is not further increased by IDH1, IDH2, or FLT3 mutations (i.e., the ~78% on-treatment ORR is the ceiling for this regimen in NPM1+/unfit/TP53-WT/non-CK patients regardless of additional mutation status).

analyses.code: within responder subgroup AND ven-aza=1, ORR by additional mutations
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup with ven-aza=1 (n=1790), ORR is ~78–80% across IDH1±, IDH2±, FLT3-ITD±, FLT3-TKD± strata, with no stratum significantly higher than the overall 78.6%. The treatment effect saturates within the defined subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL best-supported treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis for objective_response: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases ORR by approximately +63 absolute percentage points (from ~16% to ~79%) only in patients meeting all of: npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this subgroup the average treatment effect of venetoclax-azacitidine is indistinguishable from zero.

FINAL hypothesis: none of the other treatments (treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, treatment_7plus3) has a clinically or statistically meaningful positive effect on objective_response in any subgroup we could identify.

analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (npm1+ AND unfit+ AND tp53- AND CK-, n=4531): ORR(ven-aza)=78.6% vs ORR(off)=15.8%; TE=+0.628, Fisher p≈0. Outside subgroup (n=45469): TE=-0.003, p=0.42. The composite predicate fully accounts for ven-aza heterogeneity and identifies the responder population.

Across marginal tests, biomarker-matched stratifications (FLT3 inhibitors in FLT3-ITD+, IDH inhibitors in IDH1+/IDH2+), and exhaustive 2-feature subgroup searches, no other treatment showed an absolute response benefit of even +0.05 with p<0.01 in a clinically interpretable subgroup. midostaurin/gilteritinib/ivosidenib/enasidenib/7+3 all behave like inert agents in this dataset on objective response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sensitivity check: removing the responder subgroup from the data abolishes the marginal positive association of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine with objective_response and leaves the marginal effect of unfit_for_intensive close to zero.

analyses.result_summary: After excluding the (NPM1+ AND unfit+ AND TP53- AND CK-) subgroup: marginal ven-aza ORR = 16.6% vs 16.7% (TE=-0.003, p=0.42); marginal unfit_for_intensive ORR ≈ same as fit. Both 'main effects' from iterations 4 and 6 reflected the venetoclax-azacitidine response inside this small composite subgroup, not pervasive treatment or fitness effects.
